title: Amino Acid Transmitters
author_string: Cooper, Jack R, Bloom, Floyd E, Roth, Robert H
abstract:
Abstract
               On the basis of their functional actions, amino acid transmitters have been divided into two general categories: excitatory amino acid transmitters (glutamate [Glu], aspartate [Asp], cysteate, and homocysteate), which depolarize neurons in the mammalian central nervous system (CNS), and inhibitory amino acid transmitters (y-aminobutyric acid [GABA], glycine [Gly], taurine, and /3-alanine), which hyperpolarize mammalian neurons. A few amino acids have been demonstrated to fulfill most of the criteria for neurotransmitter candidates in the mammalian CNS.
